records.ety: [L. conformis; con- + forma form: cf. F. conforme.]

defs: Of the same form; similar in import; conformable.
quotes: Care must be taken that the interpretation be every way conform to the analogy of faith.

records.ety: [F. conformer, L. conformare, -formatum; con- + formare to form, forma form. See Form.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conformed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Conforming.]

defs: To shape in accordance with; to make like; to bring into harmony or agreement with; -- usually with to or unto.
quotes: Demand of them wherefore they conform not themselves unto the order of the church.

defs: To be in accord or harmony; to comply; to be obedient; to submit; -- with to or with.
quotes: A rule to which experience must conform.

defs: To comply with the usages of the Established Church; to be a conformist.
quotes: About two thousand ministers whose consciences did not suffer them to conform were driven from their benefices in a day.
